user_query:
I need to build a short, validated stats quiz for class using our eSports scrim log and also prep club follow-ups.

Please do all of the following using only the files under input/:
1) Test writing and validation (follow input/assignment_brief.md):
   - Read input/match_logs.csv and generate exactly 6 multiple-choice questions (A–D) that are fully answerable from the CSV. Include a mix of direct-fact and aggregate/rate questions per the brief.
   - Save the quiz to output/quiz/quiz.json with, for each question: id (Q1–Q6), stem, choices (A–D), correct, type (direct|aggregate), and evidence that cites supporting match_id(s) and/or groupings and briefly explains the calculation.
   - Save a compact answer key to output/quiz/answer_key.csv (columns: id, correct).
   - Produce a validation report at output/validation/report.json confirming each correct answer can be recomputed from input/match_logs.csv and the evidence you provided. Include, per question: id, computed_answer (the value you derived directly from the CSV), matches_correct (true/false), and a short note; also include an overall all_passed boolean.
   - Also compute and save a stats summary to output/stats/summary.csv with one row per group (game and map). Columns: group_type (game|map), group_name, matches, wins, losses, win_rate (0–1), kills, deaths, kd_ratio.

2) Meeting notes/action items:
   - Read input/meeting_transcript.md and extract actionable items. Create a concise notes file at output/meeting/notes.md listing each action item with: task, owner (if mentioned), due_date (if mentioned), and brief context (a 1-line summary referencing the original line). If owner or due_date is missing, set it to "TBD".

3) Message rewrite for tone/clarity:
   - Read input/email_draft.txt and rewrite it to be concise, respectful, and clear for a teacher/coach. Keep it 120–160 words, include a subject line, remove slang and any dismissive comments about retro games, and keep the request (permission to use team data for a stats quiz and share results) intact. Save to output/communication/email_rewrite.txt.

Notes/constraints:
- Use only the provided CSV and docs; do not fabricate data.
- Ensure every quiz answer is internally consistent with input/match_logs.csv and your stats summary.
- All output files must be created in the specified paths so I can verify by re-running calculations against the CSV. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
